name: Lunar and Planetary Laboratory (LPL)
relevance: An interdisciplinary institution that is a world leader in planetary science and spacecraft missions, including OSIRIS-REx and Phoenix Mars.

name: Arizona Space Institute (ASI)
relevance: Coordinates and facilitates large-scale space research projects and missions across the University of Arizona.

name: Steward Observatory
relevance: The research arm of the Department of Astronomy, managing world-class telescopes and conducting breakthrough research in astrophysics.

name: Space4 Center
relevance: Dedicated to space domain awareness, tracking objects in cislunar space, and developing astrodynamics and space surveillance technologies.

name: Space and Terrestrial Robotic Exploration (SpaceTREx) Laboratory
relevance: Develops CubeSats, planetary robots, and enabling technologies for space mining and exploration.

name: Richard F. Caris Mirror Laboratory
relevance: Produces the world's largest and most advanced telescope mirrors for missions like the Giant Magellan Telescope.

name: Arizona Astrobiology Center (AABC)
relevance: A hub for interdisciplinary research on the origins and distribution of life in the universe.

name: Arizona Space Grant Consortium
relevance: Part of a NASA-funded network that supports student research, internships, and outreach in space-related STEM fields.

name: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space (SEDS) - UA Chapter
relevance: Engages students in space science projects, CubeSat development, and professional networking in the space industry.

name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) - UA Chapter
relevance: Provides professional development and hands-on projects for students pursuing careers in aerospace and astronautics.

name: Astronomy Club
relevance: Offers students opportunities to learn about astronomy through star parties, outreach, and research presentations.

name: Wildcat Rocket Engineering Club
relevance: Focuses on designing, building, and launching high-powered rockets for competition and research.

name: Near Space Club
relevance: Conducts high-altitude balloon experiments to reach the stratosphere for scientific and engineering payloads.

name: STAR-X (Space Technology & Advanced Robotics for Exploration)
relevance: A student-led team that designs and tests autonomous robotic systems for planetary exploration missions.

name: Wildcat Space Research & Design Club (NASA RASC-AL)
relevance: Competes in NASA's RASC-AL (Revolutionary Aerospace Systems Concepts Academic Linkage) competition to design future space missions.

name: SMART Lab (Scalable Move and Resilient Transversability Lab)
relevance: Research focuses on autonomous decision-making and human-robotic interaction, often applicable to robotic space exploration.

name: Wildcat Robotics (BattleBots)
relevance: Designs and builds combat robots, fostering skills in mechanical and electrical engineering relevant to aerospace robotics.

name: Arizona Autonomous Vehicle Club (AAVC)
relevance: Develops self-driving vehicle systems, emphasizing sensors and navigation logic used in planetary rovers.

name: Student Optics Chapter (SOCk)
relevance: Promotes optical science, which is critical for the development of astronomical telescopes and space-based imaging systems.

name: Spacewatch
relevance: A long-running program dedicated to the discovery and tracking of asteroids and comets in the solar system.

name: Catalina Sky Survey (CSS)
relevance: A NASA-funded project based at LPL that discovers and tracks Near-Earth Objects (NEOs).

name: Flandrau Science Center & Planetarium
relevance: Serves as the university's public face for space science, offering planetarium shows and space-themed exhibits.

name: Boundary Layer Stability and Transition Laboratory
relevance: Conducts research on hypersonics and high-speed aerodynamics, essential for re-entry vehicles and advanced aerospace craft.

name: HiRISE (High Resolution Imaging Science Experiment)
relevance: The operations and data processing center for the highest resolution camera ever sent to another planet (Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter).

name: Kuiper-Arizona Laboratory for Astromaterials Analysis (KALFAA)
relevance: A state-of-the-art facility for analyzing extraterrestrial samples, including those from Apollo and OSIRIS-REx missions.

name: Planetary Image Research Laboratory (PIRL)
relevance: Provides computing and image processing support for planetary missions and research at LPL.

name: Wildcat Marine Robotics
relevance: Develops autonomous underwater vehicles; while primarily marine, the robotics technology is applicable to future missions to icy ocean worlds like Europa.

name: Women in Physics and Astronomy
relevance: A student group promoting inclusion and professional development for women in physics and astronomy fields.
